title: Fertilizer composition comprising a zeolite and basic l-amino acid
abstract: Thepresent invention relates to the promotionof the growth of at least one plantby providing a fertilizer compositioncomprisingat least one zeolite,into the pores of which at least one basic L-amino acid has been adsorbed; adding the fertilizer composition to a medium for plant growth; and culturing a plant therein. The invention will provide the release of nitrogen from the fertilizer composition during culture of the plant at a rate which is advantageously used bythe plant. The invention also relates toa fertilizer compositionas such, optionally combined with a medium for plant growth to make up a growth-promoting material. The invention is advantageously used to reduce the resources required in the fertilization of slowly growing plants, where repeated supply of nutrients as well as leakage of nitrogen amounts to large coststo society.
claims: Claims 1. A method of promoting the growth of at least one plant, which method comprises a) Providing a fertilizer composition comprising at least one zeolite, into the pores of which at least one basic L-amino acid has been adsorbed; b) Adding the fertilizer composition to a medium for plant growth in connection with plantation; c) Providing for the release of nitrogen from the fertilizer composition during subsequent culture of the plant. 2. A method according to claim 1, wherein the basic L-amino acid is selected from the group consisting of L-arginine and L-lysine. 3. A method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein one or more additional growth- promoting components are added. 4. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the zeolite is a natural zeolite. 5. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the zeolite(s) has a charge of at least 1%, such as at least 2% or at least 3% of nitrogen originating from said basic L-amino acid(s), calculated per total weight of charged zeolite. 6. A method according to any one of the claims 1-5, wherein the plant is a tree. 7. A method according to claim 6, wherein the tree is a conifer tree, such as a member the family Pinaceae, e.g. a member of the genera Pinus or Picea. 8. A method according to any one of the claims 1-5, wherein the plant is a grass. 9. A method according to claim 8, wherein the grass is a member of the family Poaceae. 10. A growth-supporting material, which material comprises a medium for plant growth combined with at least one zeolite, into the pores of which at least one basic L-amino acid has been adsorbed, optionally together with other growth-promoting components. 11. A growth-supporting material according to claim 10, which is provided in a biodegradable container. 12. A growth-supporting material according to claim 10, which is a pad arranged for the plantation of seeds. 13. Use of at least one zeolite, into the pores of which at least one basic L-amino acid has been adsorbed, optionally together with other growth-promoting components, as a fertilizer. 14. Use according to claim 13, wherein a growth-supporting material according to any one of claims 10-12 is combined with at least one plant. 15. Use according to claim 13 or 14, wherein the zeolite has a charge of at least 1%, such as at least 2% or at least 3% of nitrogen originating from said basic L-amino acid(s), calculated per total weight of charged zeolite. 16. Use according to any one of claims 13-15, wherein the plant is a conifer tree, such as a member of the family Pinaceae, e.g. a member of the genera Pinus or Picea. 17. Use according to any one of claims 13-15, wherein the plant is a grass, such as a member of the family Poaceae. 18. Use according to any one of claims 13-17 in the culture of at least one mycorrhizal plant. 19. Use according to any one of claims 13-18, wherein the plant is capable of symbiotic association with of a fungus. 20. Use according to claim 19, wherein the fungus produces at least one edible mushroom, such as a chanterelle.
